subject: [PATCH v5 2/2] mm: kick writeback flusher for IOCB_DONTCACHE with targeted dirty tracking

2026-05-04.patch_summary: This patch improves the performance of the IOCB_DONTCACHE writeback path by moving writeback submission off the writer's hot path and into a background flusher. The patch introduces a new WB_start_dontcache bit that uses targeted dirty tracking to determine how many pages to write back, preserving I/O batching while limiting the scope of expedited writeback. This results in significant improvements in throughput and tail latency for sequential writes, as well as improved performance in multi-writer scenarios.
